userStories.title: Initialize Vite and TypeScript workspace
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a clean Vite and TypeScript project so that the application can be developed locally, built for production, and extended safely.
acceptanceCriteria: `npm install` completes successfully
`npm run dev` starts the Vite development server
`npm run build` creates a production bundle
`npm run preview` serves the built bundle locally
`package.json` contains `dev`, `build`, and `preview` scripts
TypeScript strict mode is enabled
The default page loads in a browser without console errors
userStories.notes: Foundation ticket. No backend folders or server code in v1.

userStories.title: Add linting, typecheck, and unit test tooling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want baseline quality tooling so that all future tickets can be verified consistently before completion.
acceptanceCriteria: `npm run lint` exists and runs ESLint
`npm run typecheck` exists and runs TypeScript in noEmit mode
`npm run test` exists and runs Vitest
At least one sample unit test passes
All scripts run without interactive prompts

userStories.title: Create full-screen application shell
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a full-screen page shell so that the generated fake website feels immersive and uncluttered.
acceptanceCriteria: Viewport fills the browser window
A dedicated generated-site container exists
A separate overlay UI container exists
No default scrollbars appear on normal desktop resolutions
Browser resize updates layout without page reload
userStories.notes: Base structure for all later UI work.

userStories.title: Implement deterministic seeded random utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a deterministic seeded random system so that identical seeds always generate identical websites.
acceptanceCriteria: The same seed always produces the same random sequence
Different seeds produce different random sequences
Seed values are URL-safe strings
Generator modules do not call `Math.random()` directly in v1
A helper exists for deriving repeatable sub-seeds or sub-streams
Utility has unit tests

userStories.title: Read and write seed from URL parameters
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the website state encoded in the URL so that refreshing or sharing the page recreates the exact same generated website.
acceptanceCriteria: The query parameter key is exactly `seed`
If `?seed=` exists, the application uses that seed
If `seed` is missing, a valid seed is generated on first load
The generated seed is written into the URL using history APIs without full page reload
If an invalid seed is provided, it is replaced with a valid seed
Refreshing the page with the same seed reproduces identical metadata, theme, layout, and decorative details
Opening the same seeded URL in another browser session reproduces identical output
userStories.notes: Foundation for reproducibility, refresh, and sharing.

userStories.title: Create alternate timeline dataset definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want structured datasets for eras, archetypes, regions, vocabulary, and design cues so generated websites feel varied and plausible.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 eras exist
At least 8 site archetypes exist
At least 3 regional flavors exist including Germany
Era records include year range, design cues, and vocabulary cues
Archetype records include label, compatible eras, and compatible regions
All datasets are strongly typed
userStories.notes: No paid APIs or runtime data fetching.

userStories.title: Generate believable fake names
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want generated names that feel historically plausible so the fake websites feel authentic rather than random.
acceptanceCriteria: Name generation uses the seeded random utility
Names vary by archetype such as airline, software vendor, fan page, or startup
German-flavored names can appear naturally
Generated names avoid obvious parody or close imitation of famous brands
At least 20 snapshot tests pass using fixed seeds

userStories.title: Generate fake site metadata
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want each generated website to have believable metadata so it feels like a real historical website.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated metadata includes `siteName`, `tagline`, `yearFounded`, `statusLabel`, `closureReason`, and `summary`
The selected year is compatible with the chosen era
`summary` is short homepage-ready copy
Metadata generation is deterministic from seed
Metadata generation is independent from DOM rendering

userStories.title: Create style theme mapper by era
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want each fake website to visually match its time period so the alternate timeline feels convincing.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 visual themes exist
Theme selection is deterministic from seed and site context
A theme object defines typography, palette, spacing, borders, and surfaces
Themes include examples such as 90s table web, Web 2.0 glossy, startup minimalism
Renderer consumes typed theme objects rather than scattered inline styles

userStories.title: Render base generated homepage structure
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a complete fake homepage generated from the active seed so the concept is immediately understandable.
acceptanceCriteria: Rendering uses the current URL seed
A single-page homepage is rendered in v1
Page includes header or navigation area
Page includes hero area
Page includes footer area
No runtime errors occur during render

userStories.title: Render reusable homepage content modules
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the homepage to contain believable body content so each generated site feels substantial and unique.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 reusable body content modules exist
Examples include features grid, news block, download panel, testimonials, guild roster, route map, or pricing table
At least 2 body modules render per generated page
Module selection is deterministic from seed
Modules follow the active theme
userStories.notes: Split from original large rendering ticket.

userStories.title: Add abandoned and broken historical details
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want subtle signs of abandonment so the museum concept becomes clear after first glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Each site includes 2 to 5 deterministic abandonment details
Examples include outdated copyright years, stale news, broken badges, dead counters, deprecated browser notices, missing assets placeholders
Main content remains readable and usable
No real network failures are required to simulate broken details

userStories.title: Create floating info button
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a floating info button so I can understand what 404 Museum is.
acceptanceCriteria: Button is fixed in the bottom-right corner
Button remains visible across viewport sizes
Click opens an info panel or modal
Panel explains the concept concisely
Panel can be closed

userStories.title: Create floating refresh button
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a refresh button so I can instantly generate another alternate timeline website.
acceptanceCriteria: Button is visible near the info button
Click generates a new valid seed
The page rerenders without using browser reload
The URL updates to the new seed
No duplicate controls or stale UI remain after repeated clicks

userStories.title: Create floating share button
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to share the exact generated website I am viewing so others can open the same alternate timeline page.
acceptanceCriteria: Button is visible near the floating controls
Primary share payload is the current full URL including `seed`
If Web Share API is available, it is used
If Web Share API is unavailable or fails, clipboard copy is attempted
Visible success or error feedback is shown

userStories.title: Add responsive mobile support
userStories.description: As a mobile visitor, I want the generated websites and floating controls to remain usable on smaller screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Application is usable from 320px viewport width upward
No horizontal overflow on common phone widths
Floating controls remain tappable and non-overlapping
Typography remains readable without pinch zoom
Layouts may remain retro but must remain functional

userStories.title: Add smoke and snapshot tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want regression tests so deterministic generation and main user flows remain stable over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshot tests cover metadata generation, name generation, and theme selection
Smoke test covers first load without seed
Smoke test covers first load with seed
Smoke test covers refresh button flow
Smoke test covers share-link reproduction flow
All CI scripts pass

userStories.title: Deploy static production build
userStories.description: As a creator, I want the project deployed cheaply as a static website so it can be shared publicly without backend cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Production build succeeds
Deployment target uses static hosting only
No server-side rendering or serverless functions are required
Opening seeded URLs directly in production works correctly
Deployment steps are documented
